sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
MOUSE on the purple indicator didn't change id=12's color (still white). The click might have missed the indicator. Let me look more carefully at the indicator positions.
Looking at the image, the indicators are at the top-right. There are two small squares: one white and one purple. Let me find their exact positions by checking the segmentation for nodes in that region.
Also, SPACE didn't do anything because id=12 is still white (not purple).
Let me find the exact position of the purple indicator and click it properly.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find the purple indicator's exact position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All nodes in top region:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    if b[0][0] < 15:  # top 15 rows
        print(f"  id={n['id']}: color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} boundary={b}")

# The purple indicator should be a small purple square in the top-right
# Let me find it and click its center
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] < 30:
        b = n['boundary']
        # Estimate center
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
        center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
        print(f"\nPurple indicator center: row={center_row}, col={center_col}")
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_row, 'col': center_col}])
        break

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter MOUSE:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in [11, 12, 13]:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"  id={n['id']}: color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} top_left={b[0]}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

</parameter>

- id=7: color=p (purple), 9px, boundary=[[3, 42], [3, 44], [5, 44], [5, 36]] - wait, the last point is [5, 36]? That can't be right for a 3x3 square. Let me assume it's [[3, 42], [3, 44], [5, 44], [5, 42]].
